metadata.split_role: Expanded red/yellow, blue-staining, red-pored, and caution-lane bolete records.
metadata.notes: Physical data split only. Species record bodies were preserved; each canonical slug appears in exactly one base mushroom file. v4.2.84 updated 25 red/caution bolete records with conservative notes, sources, and explicit remaining review reasons. v4.2.86: optional photo patches are treated as fill-only for existing base-record image fields; direct image fallback hardened for Black Velvet Bolete and Suillus tomentosus where applicable. v4.2.87-r2026-04-30-mushroom-suillus-leccinum-credibility4: carried forward with patch-precedence-safe image handling. v4.2.88: 25-record mushroom credibility/review pass; porcini/Tylopilus/Xerocomus records rechecked and remaining red/caution records improved while preserving true image/local-taxonomy review flags. v4.2.96: large red-caution source split into 3 chunks of 13 records; record bodies preserved and original order retained. v4.2.97 reviewed first 25 remaining red-caution Needs Review records after the file split; stale image-only flags were cleared only where 2+ genuinely distinct real image URLs existed, one-photo/placeholder records stayed in review, taxonomy flags were preserved, and duplicate food/caution language was cleaned.

review_reason_audit.rule: Normalize explicit review signals into review_reasons/review_note and prefix notes for Needs Review list visibility.

metadata.split_note: v4.2.96 split the red-caution bolete source into three real source files. The original filename is now part 1; parts 2 and 3 continue the same record order.

review_pass_notes.rule: Clear stale image-only review only when at least two distinct real source-image URLs exist; keep one-photo and placeholder records in review.

records.culinary_uses: Not recommended as a food target in this guide because of confusion with similar red/yellow blue-stainers.
records.notes: Needs review: needs source links and needs species-specific notes review.

mushroom_profile.summary: Frequently misidentified oak/hardwood bolete; red-brown cap, yellow pores that blue, and yellow stem flushed red below.
research_notes: Updated in v4.2.84 with conservative field-guide wording and source links.

records.mushroom_card_note: Frequently misidentified oak/hardwood bolete; red-brown cap, yellow pores that blue, and yellow stem flushed red below.
records.field_identification: Red to reddish-brown cap, bright yellow young pores bruising blue, yellow stem flushed red over the lower half, moderate blue bruising, and oak/hardwood association. Compare with bicolor and sensibilis.

records.culinary_uses: Avoid in this guide. Red-pored blue-staining boletes are not beginner targets, and species boundaries are messy.

mushroom_profile.summary: Red/orange-pored, blue-staining bolete group; included for caution and comparison.

records.edibility_detail: Treat as not recommended; high confusion risk with toxic red-pored boletes.
records.field_identification: Red to orange pore surface, strong blue staining, reticulate or patterned stem, and hardwood association place it in the high-caution red-pored bolete lane.
records.mushroom_card_note: Lurid Bolete group: red/orange-pored, blue-staining bolete; not a beginner edible and not something to toss casually in a pan.

records.culinary_uses: Not a food target in this guide; retained for identification and caution comparison.
records.notes: Needs review: needs real field photo / image coverage, needs species-specific notes review, and needs source links.

mushroom_profile.summary: Neoboletus-type red/yellow-pored blue-staining bolete; high-caution comparison record.

records.mushroom_card_note: Yellow/red-pored blue-staining Neoboletus-type record; keep in the caution lane until identity is rock solid.
records.field_identification: Check for yellow to red pore tones, rapid blue staining, yellow flesh, and Neoboletus-style red-pored bolete traits. This is a microscope/taxonomy-heavy neighborhood.

records.culinary_uses: Not a food target in this guide; retained for identification and caution comparison.

records.mushroom_card_note: Red-pored blue-staining Neoboletus comparison species; useful mainly for keeping red-pored boletes out of the easy-edible bucket.
records.field_identification: Look for red/orange pore surface, blue bruising, yellowish flesh, and red-pored bolete build. Treat name-level ID conservatively.

records.mushroom_card_note: Piedmont Bolete: Caloboletus-style caution species; included for comparison, not for the skillet.
records.field_identification: Caloboletus records often involve pale to yellow pore surfaces, reticulate stems, bitter or unpleasant taste, and confusion with edible boletes; treat this one conservatively.
records.edibility_detail: Avoid unless independently confirmed; kept as a caution/comparison record.

records.general_notes: Use this entry as a warning bucket for red-pored, blue-staining boletes until individual species are confidently separated.
records.edibility_detail: Avoid for food in this guide; keep as an identification/caution record.
records.field_identification: Brown to olive-brown cap, red to reddish-orange pores, yellow/red stem without strong reticulation, blue to blackish bruising, and velvety or fuzzy lower stem/base under hardwoods.

use_links.notes: Red pores, blue staining, hardwood association, and fuzzy base traits.
